methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $106M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
ARX Robotics is a Series A-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Marc Wietfeld that has raised $106M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers operating in the same sector at same or adjacent stages, including later-stage companies such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $106M total raised as a value proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, implying a $212M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies. The peer set has a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M to $3.0B. The five named peers (Dream Security at $3.0B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, Code Metal at $1.2B, Castelion and Defense Unicorns each at $1.0B) illustrate the upper concentration of the distribution.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $180M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $606M. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty around ARX's technical differentiation, customer traction, and defense sector funding momentum relative to the peer cohort. Stage-progression yields a more conservative estimate anchored to capital deployed, while peer-median captures sector-wide valuation expansion in defense tech, making the median estimate the most balanced point estimate.
